title: Air transport, registered carrier departures worldwide
definition: Registered carrier departures worldwide are domestic takeoffs and takeoffs abroad of air carriers registered in the country.

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization:
Civil Aviation Statistics of the World, International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO), uri: https://data.icao.int/newdataplus/#:~:text=ICAO%20data%20is%20comprised%20of,information%20about%20commercial%20air%20carriers;
ICAO Staff estimates, International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO), uri: https://data.icao.int/newdataplus/#:~:text=ICAO%20data%20is%20comprised%20of,information%20about%20commercial%20air%20carriers

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include United States, China, India, while the lightest include Korea, Dem. People's Rep., Niger, Malawi; that contrast is the fastest way to read the air carrier departures pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Registered air carrier departures can reveal transport connectivity and hub geography.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Air passengers carried can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Air freight can tempt players because it shares a strong country ranking with this map, but its unit and definition point to a different story.
Cereal production overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
editorial.dataCaveat: Raw departures favor larger countries and aviation hubs.
regionalSignals: Connectivity maps often expose infrastructure, income, and island-state outliers.
Small high-income countries can be powerful probes.

reviewNotes: Batch 3 QA: useful aviation-network comparison map.
Expert-only and keep separate from air passengers and air freight in generated choices.
